LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS SUSAN HANDELMAN AND ROPERS, MAJESKI, KOHN & BENTLEY PC'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED IN PART AND OVERRULED IN PART. THE DEMURRER TO THE SIXTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF FIDUCIARY DUTY IS OVERRULED. THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT ("FAC") SUFFICIENTLY PLEADS THE ELEMENTS OF BREACH OF FIDUCIARY DUTY. (PIERCE V. LYMAN (1991) 1 CAL.APP.4TH 1093, 1101.) THE FAC ALLEGES THAT (1) DEFENDANTS WERE PLAINTIFFS' APPELLATE ATTORNEYS (FAC, 34) AND THEREFORE OWED A FIDUCIARY DUTY; (2) DEFENDANTS BREACHED THIS DUTY BY APPEALING, RATHER THAN FILING A WRIT, IN ORDER TO CHARGE "TEN TIMES" MORE (FAC, 76-77) IN BREACH OF THEIR FIDUCIARY DUTY TO PUT THEIR CLIENTS' INTERESTS FIRST; AND (3) PLAINTIFFS WERE DAMAGED BECAUSE OF THIS BREACH (FAC, 78). THE DEMURRER TO THE SEVENTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR FRAUD IS SUSTAINED WITH TWENTY DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. THE FAC DOES NOT PLEAD WITH REQUISITE PARTICULARITY "THE WHO, WHAT, WHEN, WHERE, TO WHOM, AND BY WHAT MEANS THE MISREPRESENTATION WAS MADE." (LAZAR V. SUP. CT. (1996) 12 CAL.4TH 631, 645.) THE MOTION TO STRIKE PUNITIVE DAMAGES IS DENIED. THE MOTION TO STRIKE ATTORNEY'S FEES IS GRANTED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND BECAUSE THE FAC PLEADS NO STATUTORY OR CONTRACTUAL BASIS FOR RECOVERY. THE WORDS "ATTORNEY'S FEES" AT PAGE 18:27 OF THE FAC ARE STRICKEN. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: TONY VAUGHN, CSR #6185

THE DEMURRER TO THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF FIDUCIARY DUTY IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. THE RELATIONSHIP BETWEEN INSURER-INSURED IS NOT A FIDUCIARY RELATIONSHIP. (VU V. PRUDENTIAL PROPERTY & CAS. INS. CO. (2001) 26 CAL.4TH 1142, 1150-51.) WHEN AN INSURER PROVIDES A COURTESY DEFENSE TO A NON-INSURED, IT ASSUMES THE SAME TYPE OF RELATIONSHIP AS ONE BETWEEN AN INSURER AND INSURED. (MOSIER V. SOUTHERN CALIFORNIA PHYSICIANS INS. EXCHANGE (1998) 63 CAL.APP.4TH 1022, 1048.) Motion To Disqualify Carlson, Calladine & Peterson Llp
Off Calendar May-21-2018 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
David Feldman, Tel. No. 1-650-714-7470 Plaintiff appearing n pro per;
Atty. Sieun Amber Lee of Rogers, Joseph & O'Donnell, Tel. No. 1-415-756-2828 appearing
for the Defendants;
Atty. Michael C. Cooper of Carlson, Calladine & Peterson LLP, Tel. No. 1-415-391-8189 appearing for the Defendant Ropers;
Atty. Samuel H. Ruby of Bullivant Houser/Bailey, Tel. No. 1-415-352-2723 appearing for the defendant Illinois Union Insurance Company.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Plaintiff David Feldman's motion to disqualify Carlson, Calladine & Peterson LLP is denied. Assuming without deciding that this court has jurisdiction to decide this motion and there will be ongoing proceedings in this case which makes resolution of the motion a matter of some consequence, Mr. Feldman has failed to show that the Carlson firm acted improperly and, even if it did, this motion must be denied as untimely. Mr. Feldman has not shown that any of the conversations he had with Melissa Dubbs in January 2014 were outside the scope of the authorization that Ms. Dubbs had good cause to believe was given by Mr. Feldman's then-attorney of record Robert Machado and agreed to by Mr. Feldman. By January 2014, although himself not an attorney, Mr. Feldman can fairly be characterized as an experienced litigant who surely knew that anything he said to opposing counsel might be used against him and others with whom he was allied in pending litigation. The motion is untimely. Mr. Feldman knew or could have known all the facts on which he bases this motion and presented them to his new counsel no later than the summer of 2014, yet he waited almost four years to bring this motion and now seeks to undo the work done by the Carlson firm for the last four years and require new counsel to learn all that was done in the last four years, all to the significant prejudice of the Carlson firm's clients. Mr. Feldman has not presented any cogent rationale for his delay in bringing this motion. "Where the party opposing the motion can demonstrate prima facie evidence of unreasonable delay in bringing the motion causing prejudice to the present client, disqualification should not be ordered. The burden then shifts back to the party seeking disqualification to justify the delay." (Western Continental Operating Co. v. Natural Gas Corp.(1989) 212 Cal.App.3d 752, 763.) Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR #8600 =302/hk

Motion To Set Aside And Vacate Orders
Off Calendar May-21-2018 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
David Feldman, Tel. No. 1-650-714-7470 Plaintiff appearng n pro per;
Atty. Sieun Amber Lee ofRogers, Joseph & O'Donnell, Tel. No. 1-415-756-2828 appearing
for the Defendants;
Atty. Michael C. Cooper of Carlson, Calladine & Peterson LLP, Tel. No. 1-415-391-8189 appearing for the Defendant Ropers;
Atty. Samuel H. Ruby of Bullivant Houser/Bailey, Tel. No. 1-415-352-2723 appearing for the defendant Illinois Union Insurance Company.
RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Plaintiff David Feldman's motion to vacate orders and judgments is denied in its entirety. While there appears to be little doubt that Mr. Feldman and those he is allied with in these consolidated cases were victims of egregious misconduct committed by their counsel of record Robert Machado, Mr. Feldman is not entitled to any of the relief that he seeks. Mr. Feldman's contention that the orders and judgments he seeks to have vacated are "void" and thus can be vacated at any time per CCP 473(d) is incorrect. This court has and continues to have fundamental jurisdiction over the parties and the subject matter of this case. Per longstanding law explained in Lee v. Ji Hae An (2008) 168 Cal.App.4th 558, the orders and judgments sought to be vacated are at most "voidable," triggering the 6 month period of CCP 473(b) and precluding application of CCP 473(d). Quite obviously Mr. Feldman failed to file this motion within six months of any of the orders and judgments he seeks to have vacated. Nor may Mr. Feldman invoke extrinsic fraud to set aside any of the orders or judgments. The law of the case doctrine requires that this court follow all the rulings in the recent Court of Appeal decision. Among those rulings is that the orders and judgments were not procured by extrinsic fraud because there was "strong evidence" that Mr. Machado was solely responsible for the evidentiary sanctions orders. The Court of Appeal also ruled that Mr. Feldman was not free from negligence because he enlisted Mr. Morrissey, who was suspended from the practice of law, to work with Mr. Machado on behalf of Mr. Feldman and the other plaintiffs. Although the law of the case doctrine may not apply when there has been a material change in circumstances, the 2017 convictions of Mr. Morrissey, Mr. Machado and Ms. McCarroll do not constitute a material change in circumstances because nothing about those convictions casts doubt on or requires reconsideration of the previously mentioned rulings of the Court of Appeal. Indeed, fairly read, in his declaration submitted on this motion, Mr. Machado still takes responsibility for the discovery sanctions orders and, also fairly read, Mr. Feldman's declaration on this motion re-affirms his participation in an arrangement where Mr. Morrissey, whom he knew to be an attorney not able to practice law, would work together with Mr. Machado on his and the other plaintiffs' behalf. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR #8600 =302/hk

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-14-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: WILLIAM RUSTEEN, ESQ., 415.352.2700, FOR DEFENDANT ILLINOIS UNION INSURANCE COMPANY; No other appearances.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT ILLINOIS UNION INSURANCE COMPANY's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS Sustained in part and overruled in part. Defendant's request for judicial notice is granted. The demurrer to the first cause of action for professional negligence is sustained without leave to amend. The demurrer to this cause of action is unopposed. The demurrer to the second cause of action for breach of fiduciary duty is sustained without leave to amend. The relationship between insurer-insured is not a fiduciary relationship. (Vu v. Prudential Property & Cas. Ins. Co. (2001) 26 Cal.4th 1142, 1150-51.) When an insurer provides a courtesy defense to a non-insured, it assumes the same type of relationship as one between an insurer and insured. (Mosier v. Southern California Physicians Ins. Exchange (1998) 63 Cal.App.4th 1022, 1048.) The demurrer to the third cause of action for breach of contract and to the fourth cause of action for breach of the implied covenant of good faith and fair dealing is overruled. The court does not decide issues of contract interpretation on demurrer. Whether the fraud and insolvency exclusions apply involves competing interpretations of those contract provisions. Order signed in open court. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Tony Vaughn, CSR #6185

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-14-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: MELISSA DUBBS, ESQ., 415.391.3911, FOR DEFENDANTS SUSAN HANDELMAN AND ROPERS, MAHESKI, KOHN & BENTLY PC; No other appearances.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS SUSAN HANDELMAN AND ROPERS, MAJESKI, KOHN & BENTLEY PC'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS Sustained in part and overruled in part. The demurrer to the sixth cause of action for breach of fiduciary duty is overruled. The First Amended Complaint ("FAC") sufficiently pleads the elements of breach of fiduciary duty. (Pierce v. Lyman (1991) 1 Cal.App.4th 1093, 1101.) The FAC alleges that (1) Defendants were Plaintiffs' appellate attorneys (FAC, 34) and therefore owed a fiduciary duty; (2) Defendants breached this duty by appealing, rather than filing a writ, in order to charge "ten times" more (FAC, 76-77) in breach of their fiduciary duty to put their clients' interests first; and (3) Plaintiffs were damaged because of this breach (FAC, 78). The demurrer to the seventh cause of action for fraud is sustained with twenty days leave to amend. The FAC does not plead with requisite particularity "the who, what, when, where, to whom, and by what means the misrepresentation was made." (Lazar v. Sup. Ct. (1996) 12 Cal.4th 631, 645.) The motion to strike punitive damages is denied. The motion to strike attorney's fees is granted with leave to amend because the FAC pleads no statutory or contractual basis for recovery. The words "Attorney's fees" at page 18:27 of the FAC are stricken. Order signed in open court. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Tony Vaughn, CSR #6185
